issues.description: Logic bug in threshold evaluation: thresholds are checked in ascending order [14, 7, 3, 1] with 'days_remaining <= threshold'. A certificate with 2 days remaining triggers the 14-day threshold (2 <= 14) instead of the 3-day threshold. The loop breaks after first match, creating wrong-threshold alerts.
issues.suggestion: Reverse the threshold order to check [1, 3, 7, 14, 30] or use 'days_remaining >= threshold' logic to find the appropriate threshold. For 2 days remaining, should trigger 3-day threshold, not 14-day.

summary: The FR-04 implementation has a critical logic bug in the threshold evaluation algorithm. When checking thresholds in ascending order [14, 7, 3, 1] with condition 'days_remaining <= threshold', certificates with fewer days remaining incorrectly trigger the largest threshold. For example, a certificate with 2 days remaining hits '2 <= 14' first and creates a 14-day alert instead of a 3-day alert. All 32 FR-04 specific tests pass, but integration tests that verify correct threshold matching fail due to this bug. The fix requires reversing threshold order to check from lowest to highest, or changing the comparison logic to find the highest threshold that is >= days_remaining.
